subject: Need the ACTUAL comfy-kitchen failure context from the Pod container log (the line surfaced so far is benign [INFO])
body:
Planning the v0.9.0 Pod batch â€” reordered to FLOOR-FIRST: bump ComfyUI v0.25.1 -> v0.26.0 first (MPI-139), then bake sage + per-card VRAM on the stable floor (MPI-145 + MPI-146, v0.10.0). The user flagged 'comfy-kitchen errors on the product Pod' as a concern I need to resolve before the bump.

The only log line surfaced so far is NOT an error:

  [INFO] Found comfy_kitchen backend eager: {'available': True, 'disabled': False, 'unavailable_reason': None, 'capabilities': ['apply_rope', ..., 'quantize_per_tensor_fp8', 'dequantize_nvfp4', 'quantize_nvfp4', 'scaled_mm_nvfp4', 'quantize_svdquant_w4a4', ...]}

That is comfy-kitchen loading CLEANLY (available:True, disabled:False, unavailable_reason:None) â€” a startup capability probe, not a failure. Also already verified: comfy-kitchen==0.2.10 is a CORE ComfyUI dep pinned IDENTICALLY in both v0.25.1 and v0.26.0 requirements.txt, so it is NOT a version-float or ComfyUI-Manager artifact (the product Pod has no Manager), and the product Pod is correctly on pinned v0.25.1.

You have the full container log + live context. Please answer (paste exact lines where possible):

1. Was there an ACTUAL comfy-kitchen ERROR/traceback anywhere in the log, or was the [INFO] line above the whole of it (i.e. comfy-kitchen got blamed by association for a benign probe)?

2. If there WAS a real failure â€” exact error line(s)/traceback. Did comfy-kitchen fail to install at boot, fail to import, or throw mid-generation? Did it actually break a gen or did the gen complete?

3. The probe lists nvfp4/fp8/svdquant quant kernels. Was the Pod attempting an fp8/nvfp4 QUANTIZED path that errored downstream? Any 'unsupported' / 'fallback' / dtype / 'not implemented for' line near a gen?

4. Which card/profile + image version was this on? (cu124 4090 vs cu128 5090; v0.8.0 / v0.8.1)

5. Anything else from the log to carry into the v0.26 bump verify â€” node import failures, kornia, memory pressure, sage vs SDPA?

Not theorizing ahead of the log (MPI-143 lesson). If comfy-kitchen turns out benign, I drop it from the plan and keep only a 'verify clean on v0.26 floor' check. Reply via mpi-message. Thanks.

recent_events.outcome: comfy-kitchen confirmed BENIGN (guilt-by-association with MPI-144 OOM). Dropped from v0.26 plan → smoke check only. Bonus: triton-missing lead carried into MPI-145 sage plan.
